7263.title: Pick 3, match 3  – **700/1**
7263.body: 3 correct numbers in the Irish lotto is 700/1 without the bonus ball. T&Cs apply.
7263.promoBlurb: Paddy Power Lotto Bets Rules Apply
7263.terms:
**Paddy Power Lotto Bets Rules:**
•	By placing a lotto bet, you are betting with Paddy Power (not with the respective Lotteries). Winnings from your lotto bets will be paid by Paddy Power.
•	All lotto bets are based on the numbers that are drawn from the respective Lotteries.
•	Bonus Ball(s) will not be included in settlement of bets, unless otherwise stated.
•	The dates and times on our Website are in GMT.
•	All lotto bets for a specific draw must be placed and confirmed before the first number is drawn. If, for any reason, a bet is placed after the first number is drawn, the bet will be void and the stakes returned, regardless of whether the bet would have won or lost.
•	Should a lottery provider (e.g. Camelot) introduce an additional draw to take place immediately following another draw, bets timed and dated for that day will stand against the first draw(s) to take place.
•	All bets are valid for the draw that is the bet is placed. The bet can be viewed immediately after placing your Lotto bet or alternatively My Bets sections.
•	If a lotto draw time is re-scheduled to another time within 12 hours of the advertised time of the draw all bets on that draw will stand unless the bets placed are late.
•	If a lotto draw time is re-scheduled by more than 12 hours of the advertised time of the draw all bets on that draw will be deemed void and stakes returned.
•	
If, for any reason, Paddy Power is unable to obtain all the details necessary to settle any market for a specific draw within 48 hours, then all bets on that market will be void.

•	Lotto bets cannot be combined with other bet type, i.e. sports bets.

•	The maximum pay-out for any bet, series of bets from a single source, syndicate or customer is; Irish Lotteries £/€500,000; Uk 49’s £/€500,000; all other Lotteries £/€300,000.

•	Maximum pay-out within each Lottery will depend on the market being bet on.

•	Odds are subject to fluctuation.

35798547.1605.comment: An interesting race with several holding leading claims and the lightly-raced HIGHLAND SHAH can come out on top now he's gained the winning thread. Lord Capulet heads the list of dangers, from Wrydcroft and King's School.

runners.comment: A regular at Newcastle and readily won another 7f handicap there in May. Ran well back on turf when second at Chester (7f, soft, 4/1) the following month, finishing a place ahead of Lord Capulet. That proved he's worth persevering with on grass and can go well again.

Built on several encouraging runs since joining this yard to score in an 11-runner handicap over C&D (good to firm) 25 days ago. Has to prove as effective off this higher mark but has claims all the same.
Won a Redcar maiden and Newcastle novice from 4 starts last season but has yet to kick on since handicapping and is hard to recommend at present.

Has been thriving this year, gaining a fifth success of the campaign in a 7f Redcar handicap in May. Placed on last 2 starts, latest when third at Chester last month (one place behind King's School). Looks sure to go well again in current form.

Won over C&D on 2025 reappearance but has struggled for consistency since, edging down the weights as a result. Has the hood discarded but needs to show some spark before he's of immediate interest from a betting perspective.

Landed 6f Newbury novice last July and faced stiff task in listed race there on final juvenile outing. Made no impression on handicap debut at Leicester on return and appeals as being too high in the weights at present.

Returned to her best with a straightforward success over C&D in June under a 7-lb claimer. Failed to settle when fifth of 7 in handicap (6/1) again over C&D (firm) 13 days ago. May need a few to falter to feature in this higher grade.

Won first 2 starts this year, namely at Southwell (7f) and over C&D in April. Maintained his run of form having been placed both starts since, latest when 1¼ lengths third of 11 to Alpha Capture over C&D (good to firm) 25 days ago. Has each-way claims once more.

Won 6f maiden at Wolverhampton in October. 13/2, got back on track when second of 5 on handicap/turf debut at Nottingham (6f, good to firm) 14 days ago. The booking of Loughnane is a plus for this lightly-raced sort but it remains to be seen if this longer trip will be his bag.

Free-going sort who opened his account at the fourth attempt in 10-runner handicap at Musselburgh (7f, good to firm) last month, overcoming a troubled passage to do so. Should make a bold bid to follow up after a 3 lb rise in the weights.
